editorsNote: Today's landscape is dominated by the rise of 'Agentic AI' in healthcare and a significant legal/regulatory battle for Anthropic. While Amazon and NVIDIA are pushing AI into clinical workflows, the industry is grappling with a 'trust gap' as patients remain wary of autonomous medical systems.

healthcareStories.headline: Amazon Launches 'Health AI' Assistant for Prime Members
healthcareStories.summary:
Amazon has officially expanded its healthcare footprint by launching 'Health AI,' a personalized virtual assistant now available to millions of U.S. Prime members. The tool, which integrates with Amazon's One Medical acquisition, allows users to ask complex questions about their lab results, medical records, and symptoms. For example, a user can ask the AI to explain a recent cholesterol report or seek guidance on managing flu symptoms. 

Beyond simple Q&A, the assistant provides Prime members with up to five free direct-message consultations with One Medical providers for over 30 common conditions, such as allergies and respiratory infections. Non-Prime members can access the service via a pay-per-visit model. Amazon emphasizes that the system operates in a HIPAA-compliant environment, using the nationwide Health Information Exchange to securely access patient data with explicit permission.

This move places Amazon in direct competition with OpenAI's 'ChatGPT Health' and Anthropic's 'Claude for Healthcare,' both of which launched earlier this year. The strategic implication is clear: Amazon is leveraging its massive retail and cloud infrastructure to become the primary interface for consumer health, moving beyond transactional pharmacy services into longitudinal care management.

healthcareStories.headline: NVIDIA Unveils 'OpenClaw' OS for Hospital AI Agents
healthcareStories.summary:
At its latest technology conference, NVIDIA introduced 'OpenClaw,' a new framework designed to serve as the 'operating system' for AI agents within hospital environments. OpenClaw is intended to manage the complex orchestration of multiple AI systems, handling tasks ranging from patient triage and data sorting to real-time diagnostic support. The framework aims to lower the barrier to entry for smaller clinics and hospitals by optimizing AI performance on newer, more cost-effective hardware.

Despite the technical enthusiasm—with 57% of hospital executives citing AI as their top priority—a significant 'trust gap' remains. A parallel study released during the conference revealed that 57% of patients are not yet comfortable trusting AI with their medical care. This highlights a critical challenge for the industry: while the infrastructure for 'Agentic AI' is being built rapidly, patient-facing adoption may be slowed by concerns over safety and reliability.

Market analysts now project the global healthcare AI market to exceed $56 billion in 2026. The introduction of OpenClaw suggests that the focus is shifting from standalone models to integrated 'agentic' ecosystems that can perform medical tasks autonomously rather than just providing suggestions to human doctors.

healthcareStories.headline: Algorae and Peter Mac Validate AI-Predicted Cancer Drug Pairs
healthcareStories.summary:
Algorae Pharmaceuticals has entered a new phase of its collaboration with the Peter MacCallum Cancer Centre to validate 24 high-priority drug combinations identified by its 'AlgoraeOS version 2' platform. These combinations were distilled from a massive dataset of over 500,000 potential interactions, specifically focusing on synergies between cannabidiol and other approved or investigational drugs.

The validation program will target four specific cancer types: glioblastoma, rhabdomyosarcoma, melanoma, and chronic myelogenous leukaemia. Researchers will use high-throughput screening to determine if the AI's predicted synergies translate into therapeutic efficacy in cancer cell lines. This project is a significant test for AI-driven drug discovery, aiming to prove that computational models can reliably bypass years of traditional trial-and-error in identifying effective multi-drug regimens.

techStories.headline: Anthropic Wins Injunction Against Pentagon 'Supply Chain Risk' Ban
techStories.summary:
A federal judge in San Francisco has issued a preliminary injunction blocking the Trump administration's attempt to ban government agencies from using Anthropic's AI technology. The administration had labeled Anthropic a 'national security risk' and a 'supply chain risk,' a move Anthropic argued was illegal retaliation following a contract dispute. Judge Rita F. Lin's ruling pauses the ban for at least seven days to allow for a government appeal, citing the potential for 'billions in lost revenue' if the ban were enforced.

The legal battle has exposed deep rifts between Silicon Valley and the Department of War. While the Pentagon recently signed a major deal with OpenAI, Anthropic has been fighting to maintain its enterprise and government business. Internal documents suggest that while the 'national security' label was narrow in legal scope, it created a chilling effect on Anthropic's commercial partners. 

This ruling is a temporary reprieve for Anthropic, which has seen its mobile download dominance challenged by ChatGPT in recent weeks. The company is also dealing with the fallout of a leaked internal blog post regarding 'Mythos' (or 'Capybara'), a powerful new model specifically designed for cybersecurity use cases that reportedly rattled the stocks of major security vendors like CrowdStrike and Palo Alto Networks.

techStories.headline: Meta Sets 75% AI-Assisted Coding Target for Engineers
techStories.summary:
Meta has established aggressive internal targets for its engineering teams, with some divisions expected to generate up to 75% of their code using AI tools by the first half of 2026. Internal documents reveal that in the 'Creation' organization—which manages Facebook, WhatsApp, and Messenger—65% of engineers are now required to meet this threshold. The Scalable Machine Learning division has even higher targets, ranging from 50% to 80%.

To support this shift, Meta is mandating the use of platforms like 'DevMate' and 'Metamate,' and has introduced an 'AI Performance Assistant' to help employees document their AI-driven impact during self-assessments. Janelle Gale, Meta's Head of People, indicated that AI usage will be a core component of performance reviews starting this year. 

This 'AI-first' engineering strategy coincides with Meta's massive infrastructure expansion, including an $11 billion deal for 10 new gas-fired power plants in Louisiana to power its 'Hyperion' AI data center complex. The company is also open-sourcing 'BOxCrete,' a Bayesian optimization model for sustainable concrete, demonstrating its commitment to applying AI across its entire physical and digital supply chain.

socialHighlights.content: Yann LeCun continues to advocate for 'World Models' over purely autoregressive LLMs, noting that the recent release of Google's 'Genie 3' represents a significant step toward interactive, diverse environments that can teach AI about physical reality without human-labeled data.

Andrej Karpathy observed that the 'threshold of coherence' for coding agents was crossed in late 2025. He notes that we are now in the era of 'vibe coding,' where the bottleneck is no longer the AI's ability to write code, but the human's ability to specify the desired 'vibe' or architectural intent.

Andrew Ng highlighted the importance of 'Agentic Workflows' in the latest Batch, arguing that iterative loops (plan -> execute -> reflect) are more important for performance than simply increasing model parameters. He specifically pointed to the new 'OpenClaw' framework as a sign of this shift.

googlePocItems.title: Building a Clinical Document 'Deep Thinker' with Gemini 3
googlePocItems.description: Create a RAG-based system that uses Gemini 3's 'Deep Think' capabilities to reason across thousands of medical records to identify rare disease patterns.

guide.instruction: Create a new 'Collection' in Vertex AI Vector Search to unify your clinical text data and embeddings.
Configure the collection to automatically generate embeddings for any uploaded PDF or FHIR record using the built-in semantic re-ranking.
Use the Gemini 3 Deep Think model to perform a 'reasoning-heavy' query that looks for non-obvious correlations between patient symptoms and rare genetic markers.
guide.codeSnippet:
model = GenerativeModel('gemini-3-deep-think')
response = model.generate_content('Analyze these records for signs of Fabry disease.')

googlePocItems.title: Deploying a Stateful Healthcare Agent on AWS via Vertex AI
googlePocItems.description: Leverage the new OpenAI-Amazon partnership to deploy a stateful agent that maintains patient context across multiple sessions while managed via Vertex AI Model Garden.

guide.instruction: Set up Workload Identity Federation between Google Cloud and AWS to allow Vertex AI to trigger stateful runtimes in Bedrock.
Use the Stateful Runtime Environment to define which patient variables (e.g., current medication, last vitals) should persist between chat turns.

deepLearningSpotlight.title: Recursive Language Models: Breaking the Context Barrier
deepLearningSpotlight.summary: In the March 27 edition of The Batch, the team explores 'Recursive Language Models,' a new research direction that manages context as an external variable. Traditional LLMs often lose coherence when processing extremely long documents (the 'lost in the middle' phenomenon). This new approach allows models to recursively summarize and retrieve context, effectively expanding the usable context window indefinitely. Andrew Ng notes that this is a critical step for 'Agentic AI,' as agents need to remember long-term goals and past actions without overwhelming their primary reasoning engine. He argues that the future of AI isn't just bigger context windows, but smarter context management.
deepLearningSpotlight.author: DeepLearning.AI Team

deepLearningSpotlight.title: NVIDIA's Open-Source Salvo: Nemotron 3 Super
deepLearningSpotlight.summary: NVIDIA has released 'Nemotron 3 Super 120B-A12B,' an open-weights model that is currently setting speed records for its size class. This release is significant because it marks the first time an American company has reclaimed the open-weights performance lead since Meta's Llama 4. The model is optimized for 'vLLM' inference, making it highly attractive for enterprise deployments that require low latency. Andrew Ng comments that the 'open-weights' movement is essential for preventing a monopoly on frontier intelligence, especially as proprietary models like GPT-5.4 become increasingly expensive and gated.
deepLearningSpotlight.author: Andrew Ng

generalLearningItems.title: Building Domain-Specific Embeddings in 24 Hours
generalLearningItems.summary: A comprehensive guide and recipe for fine-tuning embedding models on proprietary data (like JIRA or medical docs) using synthetic data generation. Includes a case study from Atlassian showing a 26% improvement in search recall.

generalLearningItems.title: EVA: Framework for Evaluating Voice Agents
generalLearningItems.summary: A new evaluation framework specifically for voice-based AI agents. It includes a synthetic dataset of 50 complex scenarios (like airline rebooking) to test temporal reasoning and policy following in spoken interactions.
